section_focus: Orange Core + Pipeline + Gate System (V0-STEP-4, V1-Phase 1, V1-Phase 3 pipeline sections)
note: PART2 section numbering: actual Orange Core content is in V0-STEP-4 (lines ~995-1204), V1-Phase 1 (lines ~1630-1870), V1-Phase 3 (lines ~2090-2175). PART2 sections 6.1-6.3 map to UI/UX, Rust/Tauri, Tests respectively - NOT Orange Core.

step_a.description: Forward: PART2 -> SOT. 17 factual claims from PART2 checked against SOT files.

details.claim: PART2 L1001: I-1 state transition: I1_S0_RAW -> I1_S1_PARSING -> I1_S3_READY -> I1_S4_EMITTED (S2 skipped in V0)
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 line 1078-1081: I1_S0_RAW, I1_S1_PARSED, I1_S2_AMBIGUOUS, I1_S3_READY
details.note: PART2 correctly notes S2 (AMBIGUOUS) is V1+ only. State names differ slightly (PARSING vs PARSED) but PART2 uses V0 implementation names vs SOT design names. Acceptable.

details.claim: PART2 L1097: CostGate thresholds: 70% warn, 85% escalate, 95% block (config.v1.toml cost section, P30-058 3-stage)
details.sot_ref: D2.0-07 §4.2 line 667: downshift threshold (LOCK): 80% warn / 100% block
details.note: D2.0-07 SOT defines 2-stage (80%/100%) LOCK. PART2 claims 3-stage (70/85/95). These are incompatible values. PART2 references P30-058 as source but D2.0-07 is the canonical SOT for cost policy.
PART2 V0-STEP-4 작업 내용 section matches SOT. But the AI prompt section (line 1097) contradicts this with 70/85/95.

details.claim: PART2 L1030-1047: LangGraph 5-Phase pipeline: intake -> plan -> execute -> verify -> deliver
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §2.1 line 227-232: 5-stage Perception -> Reasoning -> Action -> Memory -> Reflection
details.note: LangGraph node names (intake/plan/execute/verify/deliver) are implementation-level mappings of the SOT 5-stage conceptual pipeline. The mapping is consistent with D2.0-05 §4-7.

details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §2.2 lines 308-316: S0_RECEIVED through S8_DONE (9 states listed)
details.note: 9 states confirmed in SOT: S0-S8 with S3a_APPROVE_WAIT as sub-state.

details.claim: PART2 L1093: V0 implements 4-Gate, V1 expands to 5-Gate
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §8.1 line 2525-2535: 5-Gate system defined (LOCK) - PolicyGate, CostGate, EvidenceGate, ApprovalGate, SelfCheckGate
details.note: PART2 correctly distinguishes V0 (4-Gate in plan node) vs V1 (5-Gate with SelfCheckGate in verify node). SOT defines the full 5-Gate as target.

details.claim: PART2 L1106: Gate logic order: Policy -> Approval -> Cost -> Evidence (plan node) -> SelfCheck (verify node)
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §2.3 lines 489-491: Policy/Approval at S1-S3, Cost at S2-S3, Evidence at S2
details.note: The ordering is consistent. SOT defines positions but not explicit sequential order; PART2's ordering is a valid implementation of the SOT constraints.

details.claim: PART2 L1100: 4-Gate results combined into DecisionSchema (locked=true)
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §3.1 line 529-531: single decision principle - Decision object locked after creation
details.note: Decision locking is confirmed in SOT §3.1.

details.claim: PART2 L579: IntentFrame has 10 fields, sourced from D2.0-02 §7.1
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §7.1 (C3 section, lines 2614+): IntentFrame described but field count not explicitly stated as 10
details.note: D2.0-02 describes IntentFrame purpose and inputs but does not have an explicit 10-field schema definition. IntentFrame is not in D2.1 SOT schemas either. The 10-field count appears to be PART2's own specification.

details.claim: PART2 L581: DecisionSchema 18 fields (FREEZE) - 14 required + 4 optional
details.sot_ref: D2.1-D2 §4.1 lines 58-93: DecisionSchema with 14 required + 4 optional fields (decision_id through s_module_hints)
details.note: Confirmed 18 fields in D2.1-D2 SOT. This was previously a BLOCKER in v8 (was 17), now corrected.

details.claim: PART2 L1022-1024: I-19 Approval Manager: P0/P1 auto-approve, P2 hold, timeout 10 minutes
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §8.1 ApprovalGate lines 2537-2543: trigger on require_approval, high-cost execution. Timeout -> FB_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT -> safe exit (deny)
details.note: P0/P1 auto / P2 hold pattern is consistent. Timeout 600s (10min) matches config.v1.toml approval.timeout_s.

details.claim: PART2 L1657: 5-Gate order LOCK: Policy -> Approval -> Cost -> Evidence -> SelfCheck
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §2.3 + §8.1: Gate positions defined but not a single-line 5-Gate order
details.note: SOT defines gate positions at various pipeline stages but does not have an explicit 5-gate sequential ordering as PART2 declares (LOCK). This ordering appears to originate from CLAUDE.md §7.2.

details.claim: PART2 L1660: conversation turn limits: P0=5, P1=10, P2=20 (LOCK-AT-009)
details.sot_ref: Not found in D2.0-02 or D2.0-07
details.note: This is from VAMOS_AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC (AT-009), not in D2.0-02 directly but referenced as LOCK. Cross-verified with PART2 LOCK tracking.

details.claim: PART2 L1733: I-9 Cost Manager 3-stage alarm: 70% warn / 85% escalate / 95% block (LOCK)
details.note: Same issue as A5-A02. V1-Phase 1 repeats the 3-stage values (70/85/95) contradicting D2.0-07 SOT (2-stage: 80/100). This is internal PART2 contradiction: STEP-4 line 1009 says 80%/100% matching SOT, but STEP-4 AI prompt line 1097 and V1-Phase 1 line 1733 say 70/85/95.

details.claim: PART2 L1735: monthly limit 40,000 won (ABSOLUTE LOCK)
details.sot_ref: D2.0-07 §4.1 line 636: V1 monthly limit 40,000 won
details.note: Exact match with D2.0-07 SOT.

details.claim: PART2 L1725: FailureCodeRegistry 36 codes, FallbackRegistry 23 mappings (D2.1-D2 SOT)
details.note: Registry counts match PART2 schema seed table (line 610-611).

details.claim: PART2 L2114-2116: Circuit Breaker 3-state: closed/open/half_open, failure_threshold=3, recovery_time_sec=60 (LOCK)
details.sot_ref: D2.0-05 §4.4 (referenced). D2.1-D5/D7 schemas have 300s. DESIGN 2.0 LOCK=60s
details.note: PART2 correctly cites 60s from D2.0-05 LOCK. However there is a known SOURCE_CONFLICT between DESIGN 2.0 (60s) and D2.1-D5/D7 schemas (300s). PART2 follows DESIGN LOCK priority correctly but the schema discrepancy persists.

step_b.description: Reverse: SOT -> PART2. 12 critical facts from D2.0-02 checked for presence in PART2.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §0.3: VamosError standard - Result<T, VamosError> with failure_code, message, fallback_id, trace_id. Exception throw forbidden.
details.note: PART2 mentions FailureReport and error handling patterns but does not specify the VamosError struct or the Result<T, VamosError> pattern. Known from phase2 findings (F-03).

details.fact: D2.0-02 §2.3-B: Performance benchmarks - simple response <= 2s, complex <= 10s, self-check <= 1s, concurrent 5 requests, tiktoken <= 50ms/10K tokens
details.note: PART2 does not include these performance benchmark targets. Known from phase2 findings (F-04).

details.fact: D2.0-02 §1.2-A: Runnable protocol - all modules must implement run(), get_metadata(), health_check()
details.note: PART2 does not mention the Runnable protocol interface. Module implementations use async def but no explicit Runnable interface requirement.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §8.1: 5-Gate system (LOCK) - PolicyGate, CostGate, EvidenceGate, ApprovalGate, SelfCheckGate with specific judgment values
details.note: PART2 accurately covers all 5 gates with correct role descriptions across V0-STEP-4 and V1-Phase 1.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §2.2: 9-state machine S0-S8 with transition rules including S3 single-decision lock principle
details.note: PART2 L1739-1749 covers full 9-state machine with correct transitions.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §2.2: Self-check FAIL handling - soft loop 1 time only, then approval needed (LOCK)
details.note: PART2 L2110: Soft Loop max 1 retry (LOCK). Hard Loop: 2nd failure -> HITL approval (I-19). Matches SOT.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §4.0: I-5 maps to I-5 + I-8 (Condition & Decision Engine + Policy Engine) - 1:N expansion
details.note: PART2 correctly separates I-5 (Decision Engine) and I-8 (Policy Engine) as distinct modules.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §8.1: CostGate judgment values: normal / downshift / split / stop
details.note: PART2 references these values in the Gate pipeline implementation. However PART2 also introduces 70/85/95 thresholds not in SOT.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §8.1: SelfCheckGate - soft retry 1 time, fail -> hard loop (ApprovalGate linkage)
details.note: PART2 correctly implements this in V1-Phase 3 Soft/Hard Loop.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §0.6-A: LangGraph allowed for workflow orchestration only (DEC-002 LOCK). No nesting StateGraphs inside nodes.
details.note: PART2 L1174 explicitly states: LangGraph for orchestration only, no nested StateGraphs.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §9: Evidence(QoD) threshold - QoD < threshold means conclusion must be HOLD/ESCALATE only
details.note: PART2 V1-Phase 1 references QoD evaluation and Evidence Gate behavior.

details.fact: D2.0-02 §2.2-A: Concurrency strategy - Queue + Semaphore pattern, MAX_CONCURRENT_BLUE_NODES=3, MAX_CONCURRENT_TOOLS=5
details.note: PART2 does not mention concurrency control parameters or the Queue+Semaphore pattern.

dim_c.description: Implementation feasibility checks for Orange Core + Pipeline + Gate System

details.check: LangGraph StateGraph with 5 nodes (intake/plan/execute/verify/deliver) using START/END constants
details.note: LangGraph 0.2+ supports StateGraph with START/END. The code sample in PART2 is syntactically correct and achievable.

details.check: I-1 Intent Detector using Ollama llama3.2:3b for intent parsing with state machine logging
details.note: ChatOllama from langchain-community + structlog for logging is a proven pattern. State machine transitions can be tracked via LogEventSchema.

details.check: 4-Gate evaluation in a single plan node synchronously before Decision lock
details.note: Sequential gate evaluation (Policy->Approval->Cost->Evidence) within a single LangGraph node is straightforward. Each gate is a function call returning a result enum.
Standard Circuit Breaker pattern. Can be implemented with asyncio + state tracking. pybreaker library or custom implementation both feasible.

details.check: CostGate with conflicting thresholds (80/100 vs 70/85/95) - implementation ambiguity
details.note: PART2 internally contradicts itself on CostGate thresholds. Implementer faces ambiguity: follow V0-STEP-4 description (80/100 matching D2.0-07 SOT) or V0-STEP-4 AI prompt / V1-Phase 1 (70/85/95 from P30-058). This creates implementation risk.

details.check: A-1 MultiBrain Adapter with Failover chain GPT-4o -> Claude -> Ollama
details.note: Adapter pattern with failover is standard. Each provider has well-supported Python SDKs (openai, anthropic, ollama). 3-timeout-switch logic is straightforward.

findings.description: CostGate threshold internal contradiction: PART2 has two conflicting specifications. V0-STEP-4 description (L1009) says 80%/100% (matching D2.0-07 SOT LOCK), but V0-STEP-4 AI prompt (L1097) and V1-Phase 1 (L1733) say 70%/85%/95% (referencing P30-058). D2.0-07 §4.2 LOCK value is 80%/100% (2-stage).
findings.evidence: PART2 L1009: 'CostGate: 80%/100%' vs L1097: '70% warn, 85% escalate, 95% block' vs D2.0-07 §4.2 L667: '80% warn / 100% block (LOCK)'

findings.description: VamosError standard (Result<T, VamosError> pattern) from D2.0-02 §0.3 not reflected in PART2 implementation guide. Modules use FailureReport but the unified error handling interface is absent.
findings.evidence: D2.0-02 L34-36 defines VamosError with failure_code/message/fallback_id/trace_id. PART2 has no equivalent specification.

findings.description: Performance benchmark targets from D2.0-02 §2.3-B not included in PART2. Simple response <=2s, complex <=10s, self-check <=1s, concurrent 5 requests, tiktoken <=50ms/10K tokens.
findings.evidence: D2.0-02 L516-522 defines 5 performance benchmarks. PART2 has no performance targets section.

findings.description: Runnable protocol interface (run/get_metadata/health_check) from D2.0-02 §1.2-A not reflected in PART2 module implementations.
findings.evidence: D2.0-02 L153-161 defines Runnable interface as mandatory for all VAMOS modules. PART2 modules use async def but no Runnable compliance.

findings.description: IntentFrame field count (10) declared in PART2 has no explicit SOT backing. D2.0-02 §7.1 describes the purpose but not an exact 10-field schema. IntentFrame is not in D2.1 schema SOT.
findings.evidence: PART2 L579: 'IntentFrame | 10 | D2.0-02 §7.1'. D2.1-D1 ownership mapping does not include IntentFrame.

findings.description: Concurrency control parameters (MAX_CONCURRENT_BLUE_NODES=3, MAX_CONCURRENT_TOOLS=5, Queue+Semaphore pattern) from D2.0-02 §2.2-A not in PART2.
findings.evidence: D2.0-02 L328-337 defines concurrency strategy. Not mentioned in PART2.

findings.description: Circuit Breaker recovery_time_sec SOURCE_CONFLICT: PART2 correctly follows D2.0-05 LOCK (60s), but D2.1-D5/D7 schemas specify 300s. Schema-DESIGN discrepancy persists.
findings.evidence: PART2 L2115/2184: recovery_time_sec=60 (LOCK). D2.1-D5/D7: 300s. PART2 acknowledges this in SOURCE_CONFLICT comment.

findings.description: CostGate threshold ambiguity creates implementation risk. An implementer following the AI prompt verbatim (70/85/95) would violate D2.0-07 LOCK (80/100). The contradiction exists within the same STEP-4 section.
findings.evidence: L1009 vs L1097 in same V0-STEP-4 section. AI prompt intended for copy-paste use would produce non-compliant code.

v8_comparison.summary: v8 Agent 5 focused on schema/type system (Dim B/C). This session focuses on Orange Core pipeline, Gates, and state machine. Key overlap: DecisionSchema field count (now 18, fixed from v8 BLOCKER). New findings: CostGate threshold contradiction (HIGH), missing VamosError/Runnable/performance benchmarks (carried from phase2 F-03/F-04). v8 found 4 BLOCKERs + 7 HIGHs; this session finds 2 HIGHs + 3 MEDIUMs + 3 LOWs - lower severity reflects PART2 corrections since v8.
